sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.population: Children and adults with non-complex fractures managed in emergency or orthopaedic services.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for selected non-complex fracture assessment, documentation, imaging, weight-bearing, information, and follow-up contexts; partial for a generic fracture-follow-up workflow and not validated for UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Overview; recommendations 1.1 and 1.2
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Covers non-complex fracture assessment and follow-up, repeated pain assessment, definitive X-ray reporting, Ottawa ankle and foot rules, and selected scaphoid imaging.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.4.1–1.4.10 — ongoing orthopaedic management
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports selected orthopaedic follow-up, stability review, weight-bearing, cast or fixation context, and return review for non-improving symptoms in specified fracture populations.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.5.1–1.6.12 — documentation, information, and support
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Requires structured fracture documentation, image and report continuity, honest information, questions, treatment expectations, rehabilitation, mobilisation, weight-bearing, management-plan communication, and documentation.

archive.title: Fractures (non-complex): assessment and management — Recommendations
archive.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
